REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2795 Morgan Drive, San Ramon, California)
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Petitioner.

The sale was confirmed at $650,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $625,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-03-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Petitioner.

Ms. Boessenecker addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the number of bank accounts, the issue of the Trustee's personal account showing on the PMA bank statement, services rendered and time spent by the Trustee, the gifts (checks written) not being cashed, the gap in check numbers, the phone calls with Jeffrey and the Attorney's fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Irene Mendelson (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there was a potential for conflict of interest in appointing Jeff as Trustee. Ms. Boessenecker stated that Bruce, who resides in Nevada, and Tim, who resides in Maryland, have not shown any interest in being appointed, that if the Court wants someone else her client would step aside, that Bruce and Tim were not asked if they would serve, and that her client had informed her that Bruce and Tim wouldn't want to serve. The Court stated that there would be a better chance of avoiding a conflict if they were asked. Ms. Boessenecker stated that her client would not resign if they are appointed; she feels that they are not appropriate candidates. The Court commented that Ms. Boessenecker's client could then stay on. Ms. Boessenecker stated that her client feels Jeff is appropriate because he is assisting her now. In addition, she stated that Jeff works as a Chiropractor and that Sally works but she did not know her occupation. The Court stated that they were asking for a lot, free rent, etc. Ms. Boessenecker stated that Jeff and Sally drive Mr. Layne, cook for him, and monitor his medications. In addition, Ms. Boessenecker confirmed that the only income to the Trust is the V.A. benefits. The Court asked if Counsel was confident that the Trust won't be short of liquid funds. Discussions were held regarding the liquid funds. At the conclusion of the discussions, the Court stated that more expenses were being asked to be paid and that if it granted the petition as requested what would Jeff and Sally contribute. Ms. Boessenecker stated that they would buy groceries and pay for maintenance. The Court stated that it was not inclined to approve the request for the Successor Trustee but it was inclined to approve the accounting and the fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: David Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
